member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Configuration.HlsAudioSeekStrategy.DisableAccurateSeek
member.summary: If the video stream is transcoded and the audio stream is copied, seek the video stream to the same keyframe as the audio stream. The resulting timestamps in the output streams may be inaccurate.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Configuration.HlsAudioSeekStrategy.TranscodeAudio

member.summary: Gets or sets the remaining minutes of a book that can be played while still saving playstate. If this percentage is crossed playstate will be reset to the beginning and the item will be marked watched.

member.name: T:MediaBrowser.Model.Dlna.DeviceProfile
member.summary:
A represents a set of metadata which determines which content a certain device is able to play.
Specifically, it defines the supported containers and codecs (video and/or audio, including codec profiles and levels) the device is able to direct play (without transcoding or remuxing), as well as which containers/codecs to transcode to in case it isn't.

member.summary: Gets or sets the name of this device profile. User profiles must have a unique name.

member.summary: Gets or sets the unique internal identifier.

member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum allowed bitrate for statically streamed (= direct played) music files.

member.name: T:MediaBrowser.Model.Entities.PersonType
member.summary: Types of persons.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Entities.PersonType.Actor
member.summary: A person whose profession is acting on the stage, in films, or on television.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Entities.PersonType.Director
member.summary: A person who supervises the actors and other staff in a film, play, or similar production.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Entities.PersonType.Composer
member.summary: A person who writes music, especially as a professional occupation.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Entities.PersonType.Writer
member.summary: A writer of a book, article, or document. Can also be used as a generic term for music writer if there is a lack of specificity.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Entities.PersonType.GuestStar
member.summary: A well-known actor or other performer who appears in a work in which they do not have a regular role.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Entities.PersonType.Producer
member.summary: A person responsible for the financial and managerial aspects of the making of a film or broadcast or for staging a play, opera, etc.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Entities.PersonType.Conductor
member.summary: A person who directs the performance of an orchestra or choir.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Entities.PersonType.Lyricist
member.summary: A person who writes the words to a song or musical.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Entities.PersonType.Arranger
member.summary: A person who adapts a musical composition for performance.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Entities.PersonType.Engineer
member.summary: An audio engineer who performed a general engineering role.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Entities.PersonType.Mixer
member.summary: An engineer responsible for using a mixing console to mix a recorded track into a single piece of music suitable for release.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Entities.PersonType.Remixer
member.summary: A person who remixed a recording by taking one or more other tracks, substantially altering them and mixing them together with other material.

member.summary: Plugin load status.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Plugins.PluginStatus.Restart
member.summary: This plugin requires a restart in order for it to load. This is a memory only status. The actual status of the plugin after reload is present in the manifest. eg. A disabled plugin will still be active until the next restart, and so will have a memory status of Restart, but a disk manifest status of Disabled.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Plugins.PluginStatus.Active
member.summary: This plugin is currently running.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Plugins.PluginStatus.Disabled
member.summary: This plugin has been marked as disabled.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Plugins.PluginStatus.NotSupported
member.summary: This plugin does not meet the TargetAbi requirements.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Plugins.PluginStatus.Malfunctioned
member.summary: This plugin caused an error when instantiated (either DI loop, or exception).

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Plugins.PluginStatus.Superseded
member.summary: This plugin has been superseded by another version.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Plugins.PluginStatus.Superceded
member.summary: [DEPRECATED] See Superseded.

member.name: F:MediaBrowser.Model.Plugins.PluginStatus.Deleted
member.summary: An attempt to remove this plugin from disk will happen at every restart. It will not be loaded, if unable to do so.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

Key for this id. This key should be unique across all providers.

member.summary: Gets or sets the display name of the external id provider (IE: IMDB, MusicBrainz, etc).

member.summary: Gets or sets the unique key for this id. This key should be unique across all providers.

member.name: P:MediaBrowser.Model.Providers.ExternalIdInfo.Type
member.summary: Gets or sets the specific media type for this id. This is used to distinguish between the different external id types for providers with multiple ids. A null value indicates there is no specific media type associated with the external id, or this is the default id for the external provider so there is no need to specify a type.
member.remarks: This can be used along with the to localize the external id on the client.
